_note: Per-lesson role-play scenario. Grounded in Pre-A1/015 dialogue + vocab. Learner plays Anna; computer plays Tom. Each NPC node offers 2-3 branches so the same lesson language can be reached different ways. Consumed by RP.Engine + RP.classifiers.local (see roleplay/).
title: 015 · My School Bag

routes.meaning: Anna says the bag is blue and she likes it.
examples: It is blue. I like it.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “It is blue. I like it.”

routes.meaning: Anna asks about the new bag.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “It is blue. I like it.”

partial.message: Tell Tom about his bag: “It is blue. I like it.”

off_topic.message: Tom is showing his school bag. Try “It is blue. I like it.”

npc.text: It is new! It is blue. Do you like it?

routes.meaning: Anna says the bag is blue and she likes it.
examples: It is blue. I like it.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “It is blue. I like it.”

partial.message: Say the color and that you like it: “It is blue. I like it.”

off_topic.message: Do you like the bag? Try “It is blue. I like it.”

npc.text: I have a book in my bag.

routes.meaning: Anna asks what else is in the bag.
examples: What else is in your bag?
What else is in the bag?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “What else is in your bag?”
💬 Try saying: “What else is in your bag?”

partial.message: Ask Tom about his bag: “What else is in your bag?”

off_topic.message: Tom has a book. Try “What else is in your bag?”

npc.text: Yes, I like to read. Do you want to see more?

routes.meaning: Anna asks what else is in the bag.
examples: What else is in your bag?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “What else is in your bag?”

partial.message: Ask what else is inside: “What else is in your bag?”

off_topic.message: Ask Tom about his bag. Try “What else is in your bag?”

npc.text: I have a pencil and a ruler.

routes.meaning: Anna asks if he has a pencil case.
examples: Do you have a pencil case?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Do you have a pencil case?”

routes.meaning: Anna says she has one too.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Do you have a pencil case?”

partial.message: Ask Tom a question: “Do you have a pencil case?”

off_topic.message: Tom has a pencil and a ruler. Try “Do you have a pencil case?”

learning_objective: Say the color of your own bag

routes.meaning: Anna says her bag is green.

partial.message: Tell Tom about your bag: “My bag is green.”

off_topic.message: Tom's case is red. What about your bag? Try “My bag is green.”

npc.text: Red is my favorite! What color is your bag?

routes.meaning: Anna says her bag is green.

partial.message: Say your bag color: “My bag is green.”

off_topic.message: What color is your bag? Try “My bag is green.”

learning_objective: Say what is inside your bag

routes.meaning: Anna says she puts her lunch in it.
examples: Yes, I put my lunch in it.
I put my lunch in it.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Yes, I put my lunch in it.”

partial.message: Tell Tom why it is big: “Yes, I put my lunch in it.”

off_topic.message: Your bag is big! Say “Yes, I put my lunch in it.”

npc.text: Wow, a lunch too! Let us go to school.
